g.data-narration: Welcome to One-Dimensional Arrays. This fundamental data structure stores elements of the same type in consecutive memory locations.
g.text: Fundamental Data Structure in C Programming

g.data-narration: An array is a fixed-size collection of elements of the same data type stored in consecutive memory locations, like a row of lockers.
g.text: A fundamental data structure that stores a
• All elements must be of the same data type

g.data-narration: Arrays have four key traits: homogeneous elements, contiguous memory, fixed size at declaration, and random access capability.
Arrays are declared using syntax: data_type array_name[array_size]. The size must be a constant integer specifying element count.
Memory allocation formula: Total = elements × size of each element. For int arr[5] with 4-byte ints, total is 20 bytes.
In memory, array elements occupy consecutive addresses. Each element is spaced by the size of one element, typically 4 bytes for integers.
Arrays can be initialized three ways: complete with all values, partial with some values (rest become zero), or without size (compiler counts).
Array elements are accessed using zero-based indexing from 0 to size-1. Accessing outside this range causes undefined behavior.
Five basic array operations: traversal visits all elements, insertion adds at position, deletion removes, searching finds elements, sorting arranges them.
Linear search sequentially checks each element from index 0 until the target is found or the array ends. Returns index or -1 if not found.
g.text: Step 1: Start at index 0 → Step 2: Compare with target → Step 3: Return index if found

g.data-narration: Bubble sort repeatedly compares adjacent elements and swaps them if in wrong order, bubbling largest elements to the end each pass.
g.text: Compare adjacent pairs, swap if needed. Repeat n-1 times.

g.data-narration: In C, array name is a pointer constant holding address of first element. Pointer arithmetic allows access via *(arr + i) notation.
g.text: Array Name = Pointer to First Element

g.data-narration: Unlike simple variables holding single values, arrays hold collections. Variables access by name while arrays use indexed access.
g.text: Key: Variable access by name (x), Array access by index (x[0], x[1]...)

g.data-narration: Common mistakes include out-of-bounds indexing, forgetting zero-based indexing, and assuming uninitialized arrays contain zero values.
g.text: First element is arr[0], NOT arr[1]

g.data-narration: For exams: practice memory diagrams, trace pointer arithmetic, implement search/sort algorithms, and master sizeof calculations for array sizes.
